PayPal · Data Science Organization 2016 – 2020

Analytics Platform

Redesigning a complex risk analytics tool into an intuitive, customisable platform that gives PayPal's data scientists and analysts all account data in one place — transforming multi-tab research sessions into single-click discovery.

RedesignUser ResearchUX UIData VisualizationGraphics

The Project

From Tool to Platform

Analytics Platform is a review tool used for advanced analysis at the account level. It displays all relevant information of an account using a wide variety of graphics, and can be customised to the needs of analysts and data scientists at PayPal.

My role: Redesign this platform and give it an intuitive, effective, and aesthetic design, while maximising functionality. After the initial version launched, we expanded to a platform by adding 3 sections and 2 dashboards, according to user research insights.

The Data Science Organisation now has an interface that displays all account data in one place. Researchers are just one click away from having their desired data, in the most suitable form — no more querying data, joining tables, or filtering keys.

Analytics Platform · PayPal Data Science Organisation, 2016–2020

The Users

Power Users with High Demands

PayPal's Data Scientists and Analysts, across several geographies, with a high level of technological understanding.

🌍
Global
Cross-culture, multilingual teams
⚙️
Tech Savvy
High level of technological understanding
🎯
Varied Needs
Different work goals, habits, and priorities

User Research

Learning the Data Field

I took advantage of being surrounded by the tool's users to understand their job goals deeply, using two methods:

1

Interviews: I asked potential users to tell me about their routines, work collaborations, and give feedback on the current tool.

2

Observations: I observed users at their natural workspace, watching them work to understand how they interact with the tool, their regular procedures and habits, and what their initial "leads" were — giving me the end-to-end picture.

The Problem

Design Principles from Research

Based on user research, I mapped user types, collected pain-points, habits and requirements. This shaped three core principles:

Layout Stability

Maintain balance between the existing layout and new UI elements, so users can easily adjust to the redesigned platform.

From Generic to Customised

Most information in the older version was static and generic. Every user has unique needs — customisation is essential.

Complex Hierarchy, Simply Expressed

Hierarchy must be expressed simply — users need everything on screen without it being a burden to find the relevant piece of information quickly.

Discovery Experience

Designed for Exploration

Analytics Platform is a research tool for finding trends, behaviour patterns, and anomalies in the risk field. This field is innovative and dynamic — users typically don't have one clear flow to do their research. They need to be adaptive and creative.

The structure of the platform embodies this: users can load an account and navigate sections based on their current project needs, customising different displays according to their specific case.

Layout

The decision to design a horizontal menu — rather than a vertical one — is rooted in the need to use the full width of the screen, maximising the data and information display area.

The discovery experience starts right from the accounts-based (unique identifier) search feature. The main menu is divided into upper and lower sections, together containing all relevant data and features divided into different categories.

Information architecture: account-based search maps to the full sectioned navigation

Key Sections

Inside the Platform

Getting Started

All data in the platform is based on the search function, hence it is crucial to emphasise it to users starting from their first use.

Welcome screen: the search function is foregrounded from the very first interaction

Account General Information

The initial search result provides the general information page — an overview of the data containing what most users need at the start of their research. Sections contain different data types so users can scroll or navigate directly to their most relevant section.

Since every user has unique needs, I added an automatically-saved Drag & Move feature to customise the order of sections. Users can also expand or collapse sections to control their view.

Account General Information: all key data points, navigable sections, and map view

Account Activities

This second tab was the core of the older version. The critical pain-point was the variety of user needs — our goal was to build hierarchy that works for all users. In the older version all data was on screen without an appropriate structure.

I mapped and prioritised the data so the most relevant data-points display automatically. I used shortcuts, icons and colour-coding to give users signals for faster skimming. A colour-coded timeline on the left side gives the full picture of the page chronologically.

Before & after: from an unstructured flat list to a prioritised, colour-coded activity stream

Activities tab: shortcuts, icons and colour-coding enable faster skimming across hundreds of events

Data Visualisation

During observations, we found that users frequently export their data to produce graphs using external platforms. We wanted to be their one-stop-shop — providing integral visualisation capabilities directly within the platform.

This design decision came directly from research: users always need data in tables, yet to understand certain trends they critically need quick visual creation. The combination of these two tabs (tables and charts/graphs) allows comprehensive research work.

Linking table: accounts connected via shared IP, device, phone, address, credit card or bank account

Linking graph: visual network map showing the web of relationships between accounts

Dashboards

Dashboard views provide users with a comprehensive snapshot of performance, segmented by categories and metrics. This helps in having an overview of an account while having a better ability to recognise special cases, anomalies, and identify trends that influence future decision-making.

Seller dashboard: money flow trends, transaction breakdown, PRM scores, BBE, holds and exit metrics

User Testing

Validated at Every Step

Every single feature was examined by usability testing. I led the user testing and created interactive prototypes for users to interact with. I ensured tests were executed so users felt in their natural work zone — their own corporate computer, speaking in their mother tongue language.

Users received a specific task, while I took notes and recorded their screens for deeper analysis. This approach gave us both behavioural data and verbal feedback to refine each design decision.

Development

Pixel-Perfect Implementation

I maintained a clear line of communication with the developer by providing design guidelines, wireframes, a design document, and an interactive prototype.

I held sessions to address technical feasibility and potential obstacles, and conducted side-by-side work sessions to refine the design to pixel-perfect level.
